questions.text: The SI unit of electric charge is:
questions.explanation: Coulomb (C) is the SI unit of electric charge. 1 Coulomb = charge flowing in 1 second with 1 Ampere current.
Like charges (positive-positive or negative-negative) repel each other, while unlike charges attract.

questions.text: The electric field due to a point charge varies as 1/r^2.
questions.explanation: Electric field E = kq/r^2, where r is the distance from the point charge. It follows inverse square law.

questions.text: The SI unit of electric field is ________.
questions.explanation: Electric field E = F/q, so its unit is N/C (Newton per Coulomb). It can also be expressed as V/m (Volt per meter).
